search_queries.name: Tom
search_queries.linkedin_sector_priority: low — trades, first name only. Attempt because lead showed genuine intent (pricing question, acknowledged quote-form pain).
search_queries.fallback_query_1: site:linkedin.com/in carpentry joinery "Ripon" "North Yorkshire" owner director
search_queries.profile_verification_required: Confirm profile is clearly the owner/operator of Ripon Road Carpentry in Ripon before connecting. Do NOT connect to a generic Tom in carpentry elsewhere in Yorkshire.
search_queries.do_not_send_if: IMAP reveals any email reply since 2026-04-04 — including after the final close email
search_queries.note_type_on_find: post_reference if post found within last 60 days — write bespoke note. If no recent post: use generic note from linkedin_connection_notes.json.
search_queries.brain_status: PRESUMED_LOST_NO_RESPONSE — INTERESTED reply 2026-04-04 (pricing question, acknowledged quote-form pain). LinkedIn is last available soft channel before archiving.
search_queries.seasonal_context: May-August is peak spring/summer carpentry season. Tom may have been too busy to reply. Pain point (no quote form) is structural and has not gone away.

search_queries.name: Claire
search_queries.linkedin_sector_priority: high — salons typically active on LinkedIn and social media. Post_reference upgrade expected.
search_queries.fallback_query_1: site:linkedin.com/in hairdresser salon owner director "Leeds" "West Yorkshire"
search_queries.fallback_query_2: site:linkedin.com/in "Westfield Hair & Beauty" owner manager Leeds
search_queries.profile_verification_required: Confirm profile is Claire, owner/manager of Westfield Hair & Beauty in Leeds. Salons often have multiple staff with LinkedIn profiles — verify role before connecting.
search_queries.do_not_send_if: IMAP reveals any email reply. Claire booked a call (2026-04-07) — extra caution required. Confirm the email thread is genuinely dead. Any reply cancels LinkedIn permanently.
search_queries.note_type_on_find: post_reference STRONGLY PREFERRED. Check posts from last 60 days FIRST. Salons in May post: bank holiday appointments, summer wedding bookings, spring colour treatments, prom season, team news. Write bespoke note referencing specific post. If no post: use generic note.
search_queries.brain_status: PRESUMED_LOST_NO_RESPONSE — STRONGEST warm pipeline lead. Booked a call (2026-04-07), confirmed booking-gap pain. LinkedIn is last channel. DO NOT reference the call, the email, or any prior contact in the note.
search_queries.seasonal_context: May is peak season for salons — wedding hair, prom, bank holiday appointments. Claire may have been too busy to progress. Post-bank-holiday Tuesday is the structural optimal send day.

search_queries.name: Rob
search_queries.linkedin_sector_priority: high — B2B print/design sector well-represented on LinkedIn for client and supplier relationships.
search_queries.fallback_query_1: site:linkedin.com/in print design owner director "Sowerby Bridge" OR "Halifax" "West Yorkshire"
search_queries.fallback_query_2: site:linkedin.com/in "Pennine Print & Design" print owner manager director
search_queries.profile_verification_required: Confirm profile is Rob, owner/director of Pennine Print & Design in Sowerby Bridge. Do not connect to another Rob in print elsewhere.
search_queries.do_not_send_if: IMAP reveals any email reply. Rob's upsell signal ('sort the whole thing in one go') means a late reply is plausible — IMAP check is especially critical.
search_queries.note_type_on_find: post_reference if post found within last 60 days — B2B business posts likely (client work, events, print news, weddings/graduations peak season). If no recent post: use generic note.
search_queries.brain_status: PRESUMED_LOST_NO_RESPONSE — HIGHEST REVENUE POTENTIAL. INTERESTED reply 2026-04-04 with upsell signal ('sort the whole thing in one go' — full redesign). LinkedIn is last channel. Do NOT reference the email, the redesign discussion, or any prior contact.
search_queries.seasonal_context: May is peak print season — weddings, events, graduations. Rob may have been too busy in April. Redesign pain is still live.

hands_tuesday_2026_05_06_session_plan.description: First working day after May Day bank holiday. Warm pipeline LinkedIn session.
hands_tuesday_2026_05_06_session_plan.note: Batch 5 initial email sends also target 09:00-10:30 Tuesday. Keep sessions separate: emails first (09:00-10:30), LinkedIn second (after 11:00). Do not overlap — IMAP sweep for LinkedIn covers warm pipeline (1/3/22) only.
hands_tuesday_2026_05_06_session_plan.execution_target: 2 hours maximum for LinkedIn session. All searches, profile checks, post scans, and note sends in a single sitting.
priority_order: 1. IMAP SWEEP for leads 1, 3, 22 — mandatory first. Any new reply cancels that lead's LinkedIn note. Log in CHANGELOG.md immediately.
2. Google search for each eligible lead — run query, open profile, verify ownership.
3. Scroll posts from last 60 days — if post found: write bespoke post_reference note under 200 chars. If no post: use pre-generated generic note.
5. Write linkedin_profiles.json IMMEDIATELY after session — every lead searched, even if no note was sent or no profile was found.
hands_tuesday_2026_05_06_session_plan.post_reference_protocol: For each lead: (1) run Google query, (2) open profile, (3) scroll posts from last 60 days, (4) if post found: write bespoke note under 200 chars referencing the specific post, (5) if no post: use pre-generated generic note. Count every character including spaces and punctuation — LinkedIn free account limit is 200 chars absolutely.
hands_tuesday_2026_05_06_session_plan.char_counting_reminder: 200 chars is strict. Test in a notepad before sending. Count spaces.
hands_tuesday_2026_05_06_session_plan.failure_protocol: If HANDS cannot execute Tuesday 2026-05-06: log reason in CHANGELOG.md. Wednesday 2026-05-07 is the last acceptable day. After Wednesday: warm pipeline connection opportunity is stale — archive leads 1/3/22 and move on. Do not silently skip.
